profiles.name: Bumika
profiles.education: First-year BBA, Oxford College
profiles.label: Chatty Bumika, first-year BBA student
profiles.description: Bumika is a first-year BBA student at Oxford College who is also preparing for Company Secretary exams. She is currently studying and not yet employed, and on the call she sounds hesitant and chatty, asking many clarification questions. She wants a job-ready path that can lead to a high starting salary, hoping to shift from her current academic track into a stronger career.

profiles.name: Dvirid
profiles.education: First-year mass media, Indra University
profiles.label: Chatty Dvirid, mass media student and content creator
profiles.description: Dvirid is a first-year mass media student at Indra University, originally from a small town and helping in her father's business while running a wedding-content creator page called Shadi Memories. She is enthusiastic about communication and creative work and wants to improve her career prospects and pursue a master's abroad. She comes across as chatty and ambitious, though she occasionally struggles with technical issues on the call.

profiles.name: Indrani
profiles.education: Second-year Electronics and Computer Science, Viddylanka Institute of Technology, Mumbai
profiles.label: Eager Indrani from Mumbai, second-year engineering student
profiles.description: Indrani is a second-year Electronics and Computer Science student at Viddylanka Institute of Technology in Mumbai's Thana area. She lives with her mother, a primary school teacher, after her father passed away two years ago. She is eager to boost her future career, while being careful about costs.

profiles.name: Khushal
profiles.education: First-year BA Geography, Shahid Boggting College, Kelly University, New Delhi
profiles.label: Chatty Khushal from Delhi, first-year geography student
profiles.description: Khushal is a first-year BA Geography student from New Delhi at Shahid Boggting College, Kelly University. He is currently studying and not working, and he is price-sensitive, asking many questions about fees and value. He is enthusiastic and hoping to boost his career prospects. He appears chatty and a bit hesitant, needing to discuss things with his parents before deciding.

profiles.name: Shrenik
profiles.occupation: Runs a millet snacks D2C startup
profiles.education: Dual degrees in business management and entrepreneurship (online IIM Bangalore programme plus an offline business school in Bangalore)
profiles.label: Well-researched Shrenik from Mumbai, millet-startup founder
profiles.description: This is Shrenik, born and brought up in Mumbai, currently doing dual degrees — an online IIM Bangalore programme plus an offline business school in Bangalore — in business management and entrepreneurship. He also runs a five-month-old millet snacks D2C startup with about 4 lakh in sales across 25 retail shops. He's confident and extremely well-researched — the type to catch three different fee figures across the brochure, the website and the pitch and insist they get verified. His parents (dad runs an ice cream distribution business) are funding it.

profiles.name: Ajay
profiles.education: Distance B.A. in Economics
profiles.label: Soft-spoken Ajay from Kashmir, distance economics student
profiles.description: This is Ajay from a hilly area of Jammu & Kashmir, doing a distance B.A. in Economics; his parents are farmers. He found out about it through Insta ads and is curious but cautious — he keeps checking whether a non-tech guy can cope and whether two and a half months is really enough. Money runs through his parents, so he can't confirm anything without discussing it with them first. Soft-spoken and respectful throughout.

profiles.name: Anirudh
profiles.occupation: Runs his own gardening brand 'Mohmitti'
profiles.education: Logistics and supply chain, Chandigarh University
profiles.label: Confident Anirudh from Patiala, serial entrepreneur
profiles.description: This is Anirudh from Rajpura, Patiala. A 12th-pass commerce kid who's already a serial entrepreneur — car showroom salesman, a dealership, a failed massager e-commerce venture, and now his own gardening brand 'Mohmitti' — he has just taken admission at Chandigarh University in logistics and supply chain. He already automates his socials, LinkedIn and email outreach with AI subscriptions, and talks fast and confident, with ambitions in international trade and investment banking. He wants new skills and an internship; his parents are government employees.

profiles.name: Vatsal
profiles.education: Second-year BBA (graduating 2028), Kolkata (shifting to Pune)
profiles.label: Eager Vatsal from Kolkata, parent-funded BBA student
profiles.description: This is Vatsal, a second-year BBA student currently in Kolkata (shifting to Pune), graduating in 2028. He is doing a corporate finance internship at RPSD after a stint as an influencer manager, both landed via LinkedIn, and has a research paper pending publication. Polite, eager and fully parent-funded — his businessman father even made him decline a paid internship, and he assumes the fee is a fraction of what it actually is. Sharp with numbers though, the type to catch a GST calculation error in a fee slide — he wants a competitive edge over his peers.

profiles.name: Bala
profiles.education: Mechanical engineering, IIT (just finished third year)
profiles.label: Easygoing Bala, IIT mechanical student
profiles.description: This is Bala, a mechanical engineering student at IIT who has just finished his third year. He admits he only took mechanical for the IIT tag and has no interest in it — he wants to move into a different career, with only a bit of second-year Python behind him. Both parents are government teachers and are on board, and a friend referred him. Easygoing and agreeable on the call, barely pushes back, mainly worried about placements.

profiles.name: Harshit
profiles.education: Final-year B.Com (Hons), Gurukul Narsan, Haridwar district
profiles.label: Deferential Harshit from Haridwar, final-year B.Com student
profiles.description: This is Harshit Rathi from Gurukul Narsan in Haridwar district, Uttarakhand, a B.Com (Hons) student in his final year. He has zero internships or work experience — this would be his first step outside academics — and he wants to head straight into a finance-side job after graduating. Father runs a business, mother is a homemaker, and his dad's opinion clearly matters in the decision. Extremely deferential on the call, answering 'yes sir' to nearly everything and asking almost no questions.

profiles.name: Loknath
profiles.occupation: contractual junior engineer on the Jal Jeevan Mission (pump selection, installation, site inspections)
profiles.education: Finishing an M.Tech in manufacturing technology (final exam next month), after a 2024 mechanical B.Tech
profiles.label: Careful Loknath from Birbhum, M.Tech-finishing junior engineer
profiles.description: This is Loknath from Birbhum, West Bengal. He's finishing an M.Tech in manufacturing technology (final exam next month) after a 2024 mechanical B.Tech, while juggling a contractual junior engineer job on the Jal Jeevan Mission - pump selection, installation, site inspections. His parents are farmers who 'don't understand much' about the fees, so he's respectful but careful: he asks about prerequisites and missing classes during work weeks, and needs to confirm with his parents before paying anything. He found out about it on an Instagram reel and wants it for career growth and better opportunities.

profiles.name: Deepshikha
profiles.education: Incoming first-year BBA in commerce and international accounting (classes not yet started)
profiles.label: Green Deepshikha, incoming BBA fresher deferring to parents
profiles.description: This is Deepshikha, who just wrote her class 12 boards and is enrolled for first-year BBA in commerce and international accounting - college classes haven't even started yet. She scored above 80% and got a scholarship; her father is in service and her mother is a housewife who does social work. She's sweet but green: knows almost nothing about the fees and defers everything to her parents, even putting her mother on the call mid-session. She's looking to build a stronger career as she moves into the finance world.

profiles.name: Amit
profiles.education: PG in Hindi literature, after a science graduation in maths and physics
profiles.label: Uncertain Amit from UP, Hindi literature postgrad
profiles.description: This is Amit from UP, doing a PG in Hindi literature after a science graduation in maths and physics. He flatly describes himself as still unemployed, aiming for either the corporate sector or government competitive exams. He joins on a connection where his audio keeps breaking, and comes across as a soft-spoken, uncertain candidate rather than a motivated buyer.

profiles.name: Sushil
profiles.education: Final-year MCA student (finished BCA in 2024); knows a bit of Python and C++
profiles.label: Hesitant Sushil from Ranchi, final-year MCA student
profiles.description: This is Sushil Kumar Singh from Ranchi, Jharkhand. He is a final-year MCA student (finished BCA in 2024) who knows a bit of Python and C++ and is looking for a job. He is quite hesitant and lost, openly admitting he does not know what he can do, and his college schedule makes him hard to pin down for calls. He needs step-by-step hand-holding for even simple things like finding his way around a website.

profiles.name: Souvik
profiles.education: College student
profiles.label: Parent-dependent Souvik, price-sensitive college student
profiles.description: This is Souvik, a college student whose parents sit in with him on the call. Money is tight at home — his college's first installment fees are due, and that weighs on any extra spending. He is polite and apologetic in manner but firm once his mind is made up. Clearly parent-dependent and price-sensitive.

profiles.name: Sanjari
profiles.education: First-year BBA student; finished higher secondary at Kendriya Vidyalaya
profiles.label: Cheerful Sanjari, first-year BBA student
profiles.description: This is Sanjari, a first-year BBA student who finished her higher secondary at Kendriya Vidyalaya; college runs 7:30 to 10:30 in the morning, leaving her free after. She was raised by a single mother who is a hardware engineer, and a friend recommended it to her. She wants to broaden her skills and sees herself in a managerial role in five years. She is cheerful and cooperative, though any payment would need her mother's sign-off.

profiles.name: Pratik
profiles.education: Final-year B.Tech CSE (IoT specialization), Techno India College, Kolkata
profiles.label: Money-conscious Pratik from Jamshedpur, final-year CSE student
profiles.description: This is Pratik Singh from Jamshedpur, Jharkhand, a final-year B.Tech CSE (IoT specialization) student at Techno India College, Kolkata. He researched his options himself. He is a fitness-focused gym-goer, polite and engaged on the call, but money-conscious — his first question is the actual price, and he asks about the discontinued pay-after-placement option, EMI breakup, and exactly when he has to pay. He also probes the placement support closely.

profiles.name: Ayesha
profiles.education: Final-year B.Com, Vivekananda College, University of Delhi (MBBS dropout)
profiles.label: Eager Ayesha, MBBS dropout in final-year B.Com
profiles.description: This is Ayesha Singh from Patna, now in Delhi doing the final year of her B.Com at Vivekananda College, University of Delhi. She is an MBBS dropout — cleared NEET but walked away during counselling because she always wanted commerce and business, against her mother's wishes; her father is a real estate businessman in Bihar and Jharkhand. She is keen, well-prepped and eager — gives textbook answers and says she can make her own decisions. She wants to build her skills and grow her career in a field with strong future growth.

profiles.name: Dhanraj
profiles.education: BBA Honours student at Vikram University (just finished second year)
profiles.label: Parent-dependent Dhanraj from Ujjain, BBA student
profiles.description: This is Dhanraj from Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, a BBA Honours student at Vikram University who has just finished his second year. His father has a government job and his mother is a stock market advisor. He applied via social media wanting extra knowledge beyond what college gives. He joins from his phone, gives short agreeable answers, and is clearly parent-dependent — he wouldn't put down money without consulting his parents, and his father is away on duty.

profiles.name: Arwa
profiles.occupation: runs a small home bakery business
profiles.education: Second-year B.Com, Government Girls College; holds a diploma in home science
profiles.label: Shy Arwa from Ratlam, runs a home bakery
profiles.description: This is Arwa from Ratlam, Madhya Pradesh, a second-year B.Com student at the Government Girls College who also runs a small bakery business from home and holds a diploma in home science. She is newly married and her husband, currently out of the country, has okayed it. She dreams of becoming an entrepreneur and building her own product. She is curious but shy — she asks about fees, class timings and whether tools are paid, and specifically says she does not want to show her face in classes.

profiles.name: Mohammed
profiles.education: Second-year BBA, university in Indore
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Owais from Ujjain, second-year BBA student
profiles.description: This is Mohammed Owais Qureshi from Ujjain, Madhya Pradesh, a second-year BBA student at a university in Indore. His father is a stock broker and his mother a housewife. He saw an Instagram ad and applied just out of curiosity without really knowing what it was about. He is honest and polite but seriously price-constrained — the fee is too much for his family to afford even on EMI, and he asks about a scholarship test someone had mentioned.

profiles.name: Tirth
profiles.education: Second-year BMS, Shiv Nadar University; interning at Tech Mahindra
profiles.label: Combative Tirth from Delhi, second-year BMS student
profiles.description: This is Tirth from Delhi, a second-year BMS student at Shiv Nadar University who scored 82% in 12th with commerce and maths, currently interning at Tech Mahindra. He aspires to finance, consulting or investment banking and wants to use his three-month vacation productively after reels flooded his Instagram feed. He is articulate but combative — quick to argue, immune to seats-running-out pressure, and firm that nothing gets paid until he has discussed it with his parents, who are at work.

profiles.name: Juberia
profiles.education: First-year BSc Honours, Panjab University
profiles.label: Parent-dependent Juberia from Himachal, first-year BSc student
profiles.description: This is Juberia Khan from Himachal Pradesh, a first-year BSc Honours student at Panjab University who scored 87% in 12th. Her father runs a timber business dealing in teak and pine, and she dreams of starting her own business or startup someday after gaining experience. She came across the opportunity through Instagram reels and is keen on skill-based learning. She is sweet and willing but completely parent-dependent — her father spends his days at the shop, she hasn't told her parents about the application yet, and any spending would need his word.

profiles.name: Dhanashri
profiles.education: BS-MS dual degree, IISER Mohali (math major, data science minor), final year coming up
profiles.label: Soft-spoken Dhanashri, IISER math and data student
profiles.description: This is Dhanashri from Maharashtra, doing a BS-MS dual degree at IISER Mohali with a math major and data science minor, final year coming up. Her dad is a pharmacist and her mum a government teacher. She's soft-spoken and cooperative, and needs her parents' okay on any money before she can commit. She wants a domain in which to apply her math and data science skills, and to build a portfolio for an MBA or further studies.

profiles.name: Yadra
profiles.education: Final-semester master's in data science (statistics bachelor's behind it)
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Yadra, final-semester data science student
profiles.description: This is Yadra (name garbled in the audio), a final-semester master's student in data science with a statistics bachelor's behind it. He'd rather avoid in-depth coding and likes the bridge between the business and technical sides. He's direct and extremely price-sensitive — his budget for the whole six months is three to five thousand rupees.

profiles.name: Tanisha
profiles.education: Third-year B.Com LLB, Banasthali Vidyapith
profiles.label: Polite Tanisha from Patna, third-year law student
profiles.description: This is Tanisha from Patna, a 20-year-old third-year B.Com LLB student at Banasthali Vidyapith, about to move to Delhi for an internship. She's polite and engaged, genuinely drawn to building toward a corporate-law career. She's parent-dependent on money — nothing gets paid without her parents in the loop.

profiles.name: Advait
profiles.education: Final-year B.E. (IT) with cybersecurity minor, living in a Pune hostel
profiles.label: Compliant Advait in Pune, final-year IT student
profiles.description: This is Advait from Nagpur, living in a Pune hostel, a final-year B.E. (IT) student with a cybersecurity minor. He's skipping campus placements because he's planning an MS abroad in cybersecurity; it came recommended via his uncle's colleague. He's compliant and parent-dependent — any money would have to come from his parents. He treats it as upskilling alongside his higher-studies plan.

profiles.name: Harshita
profiles.education: Second-year BSc biochemistry, Bundelkhand University; done a 10-month event-marketing internship
profiles.label: Chatty Harshita from Jhansi, biochemistry student into marketing
profiles.description: This is Harshita from Jhansi, a second-year BSc biochemistry student at Bundelkhand University who's done a 10-month event-marketing internship. She's confident, chatty and articulate, already uses Claude, ChatGPT and Perplexity, and asks practical questions like whether tool subscriptions are included. She wants to back her marketing and storytelling ambitions with data, eyeing event or brand marketing as a career.

profiles.name: Nirmit
profiles.education: Fourth-year B.E. (IT) studying AIML, LDRP
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Nirmit from Gujarat, fourth-year IT student
profiles.description: This is Nirmit from Gujarat, a fourth-year B.E. (IT) student at LDRP studying AIML. He skipped college placements because he feels unprepared and his communication skills are weak. He's brief and very price-sensitive: he's the only man in the house, his father has passed away, and a fee around 50k is simply out of reach for him.

profiles.name: Yukta
profiles.education: Between first and second year of an MBA in business analytics and finance, Atlas (finished a BBA in international business)
profiles.label: Non-committal Yukta, MBA student padding her resume
profiles.description: This is Yukta Rajput, who finished a BBA in international business and is now between the first and second year of an MBA in business analytics and finance from Atlas. She's already done a 3-month data analytics certification in Surat and mainly wants to lift her resume and profile. She's polite but non-committal; her parents control the payment, and her dad only gets home after 9.

profiles.name: Rajshri
profiles.education: First-year BBA, Xavier University
profiles.label: Sceptical Rajshri, first-year BBA student at Xavier
profiles.description: This is Rajshri Dara, a first-year BBA student at Xavier University who just wrapped end-term exams and wants to spend her month-long holiday productively. She knows basic Excel, SQL and Python from 12th-grade computer classes and wants to improve her LinkedIn and land a paid internship next year. She's chatty, witty and openly sceptical, burned by past certificate courses — the type to quiz you on everything from PWC's full form to whether the professors are 'rough and tough'. Her mother insists on demo classes before paying for anything.

profiles.name: Vinayak
profiles.education: Third-year BA LLB, Symbiosis Law School
profiles.label: Curious Vinayak from Meerut, Symbiosis law student
profiles.description: This is Vinayak from Meerut, a third-year BA LLB student at Symbiosis Law School. He already uses ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude for legal research and drafting. He's curious and chatty, asks sharp counter-questions and how things connect to his own field, and is easily tempted to browse other offerings, like a pricier option on the website.

profiles.name: Harshal
profiles.education: Third-year (6th sem) Electronics and Computer Engineering, Pune Institute of Computer Technology
profiles.label: Agreeable Harshal from Pune, third-year engineering student
profiles.description: This is Harshal Kale from Pune, a third-year (6th sem) Electronics and Computer Engineering student at Pune Institute of Computer Technology. He's done a corporate website internship, a GenAI hackathon, and has beginner Tableau knowledge; he picked electronics due to CET marks, not interest, and wants to pivot into a different career direction. He wants to strengthen his portfolio before fourth-year placements start. He's polite and very agreeable — answers almost everything with 'no sir, no doubt' — but needs his parents' permission for any payment.

profiles.name: Harshita
profiles.education: Second-year undergrad, Punjab Engineering College
profiles.label: Sensible Harshita from Chandigarh, second-year engineering student
profiles.description: This is Harshita from Chandigarh, a second-year undergrad at Punjab Engineering College. She's been part of a venture before and now wants real industry skills to turn that interest into a career. She's an engaged, sensible questioner — asks upfront how industrially valid it is, whether it'll get her an internship or PPO, and about real projects and the fee structure. Her parents (dad a government educator, mom running a home business) know she's interested, but she'll want to discuss with them before committing money.

profiles.name: Ayush
profiles.education: Second-year BA-type course (no tech background)
profiles.label: Vague Ayush, Hindi-preferring second-year BA student
profiles.description: This is Ayush, who finished 12th in 2022 and is now in the second year of his graduation (a BA-type course, no tech background). He says he's doing nothing else right now and wants to enroll simply to learn something new, though he can't articulate much beyond that. He's more comfortable in Hindi than English and gives very short, vague answers.

profiles.name: Sahil
profiles.education: Final-semester BSc
profiles.label: Noncommittal Sahil, final-semester BSc student
profiles.description: This is Sahil, a BSc student in his final semester. He knows very little about it — he asks whether the admission is free and seems surprised it's a paid one. He's casual and noncommittal, and beyond that not much detail about him comes through.

profiles.name: Yogesh
profiles.education: Final-year BSc Biology (6th semester)
profiles.label: Hard-to-read Yogesh from Kota, final-year biology student
profiles.description: This is Yogesh from Kota, Rajasthan, a final-year BSc Biology student (6th semester). He wants to move into a better career after college, possibly via a master's, and says he's discussed it with his parents. He's hard to read — mostly short yes/no answers, and he doesn't volunteer much on his own.

profiles.name: Ruben
profiles.education: First-year BTech in material science, NIT Karnataka
profiles.label: Confident Ruben from Bangalore, NIT material science student
profiles.description: This is Ruben, originally from Kerala and now living in Yelahanka, North Bangalore; his mom is a professor at Reva University. He just finished first year of BTech in material science at NIT Karnataka and doesn't see his core branch converting into a high-paying career. He's articulate and confident, asking sharp questions about exam dates, schedule flexibility and how full the batch is, but he's parent-dependent — his mom is on board, yet he won't move on money without running it past his dad. He likes working with numbers and wants a more creative, better-paying path than core engineering.

profiles.name: Saksham
profiles.occupation: runs a print-on-demand t-shirt venture (Shopify store plus a US dropshipping site)
profiles.education: First-year BCom
profiles.label: Guarded Saksham from Ludhiana, print-on-demand t-shirt entrepreneur
profiles.description: This is Saksham from Ludhiana, a first-year BCom student who scored 91% in his 12th. He runs his own print-on-demand t-shirt venture — Shopify store, a US dropshipping site that's live but struggling, and 50-60k a month going into marketing, all single-handedly. He's confident and businesslike but guarded: his only questions are about fees and the exam pattern, and he won't put down any money without his father. He wants to be able to read his own business numbers so he never needs a consultant.

profiles.name: Jaspreet
profiles.education: Final-year BTech computer science, Bharati Vidyapeeth, Pune
profiles.label: 22 year old deferential Jaspreet, final-year BTech student
profiles.description: This is Jaspreet Singh from Kanpur, 22, in the final year of BTech computer science at Bharati Vidyapeeth in Pune — he graduates in June. His dad is an engineer at HAL in Kanpur, and any payment has to go through him. He's deferential and eager to please, says 'sir' in nearly every sentence, and complains about partiality in campus placements. His single goal is landing a good job, though he worries about classes clashing with his June university exams.

profiles.name: Ayush
profiles.education: Third-year BTech CS (AIML), Manipal University Jaipur
profiles.label: Decisive Ayush, third-year AIML student at Manipal
profiles.description: This is Ayush Shukla from Kanpur, currently at home in Gurgaon. He's a third-year BTech CS (AIML) student at Manipal University Jaipur with his fourth year starting in August. He's engaged and reasonably sharp, asks about tests, projects and offline exams, and says he can put in 10-12 hours a week. He wants a placement backup because he doesn't trust his college placements.

profiles.name: Aditya
profiles.education: Commerce-background student, Delhi
profiles.label: Detail-oriented Aditya from Delhi, commerce student mid-exams
profiles.description: This is Aditya from Delhi, a commerce-background student whose exams are currently going on. He's already done a few Udemy courses in Excel and found his way here through a Facebook ad, so the interest is genuine. He's polite but detail-oriented, full of questions about recordings, attendance rules, doubt support and who actually runs things. His parents know about the fees, but any payment depends on his father, who's travelling at the moment.

profiles.name: Kriti
profiles.occupation: Runs a small handmade gifting startup (pipe-cleaner bouquets and gift hampers)
profiles.education: Second-year college student, Guwahati
profiles.label: Independent Kriti from Guwahati, handmade-gifting startup founder
profiles.description: This is Kriti from Assam, around 18-19, a second-year college student living in Guwahati for studies. She runs a small handmade gifting startup — pipe-cleaner bouquets and gift hampers — that she's restarting with a partner, and earns a little through network marketing work on the side. She's chatty, creative and fiercely independent: her father already paid 15k for a digital marketing course so she refuses to ask him again, negotiates hard, and caps herself at 2-3k a month. She wants to scale her business in a smarter, more structured way instead of running it on instinct.

profiles.name: Anshul
profiles.education: Final-year computer science BTech, VIT Bhopal
profiles.label: Practical Anshul from Indore, final-year VIT student
profiles.description: This is Anshul from Indore, a computer science BTech student at VIT Bhopal heading into his final year. He's already dabbled in web development, model training, prediction models and chatbots. It runs in the family — his father used to work in the field (now at TVS) and his sister is in banking. He's calm, articulate and practical, asking about individual capstones, recordings and payment deadlines, though any payment would involve his dad.

profiles.name: Harsh
profiles.occupation: System manager at an IT firm
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Harsh from Agra, system manager
profiles.description: Harsh is a system manager from Agra with about two years of experience in his current role. He is cautious and repeatedly asks about costs and details, showing a price-sensitive, hesitant style. He wants to build new skills and advance his career.

profiles.name: Adithi
profiles.occupation: Software developer
profiles.label: Cautious Adithi from Shimla, software developer
profiles.description: Adithi is a developer with roughly 1.5 years of experience, originally from Shimla, Himachal Pradesh. She is currently working in software development and wants to make a career switch. On the call she seems cautious and inquisitive, repeatedly checking whether there are practical projects and how placement support works. She is also focused on meeting the attendance and fee payment requirements.

profiles.name: Vishal
profiles.occupation: Project engineer at VigPro
profiles.label: Hesitant Vishal, project engineer weighing other options
profiles.description: Vishal is a recent electronics and communication engineering graduate who has been working for about two years as a project engineer at VigPro. He is cautiously hesitant, weighing other opportunities, and comes across as price-sensitive. He wants to pivot into a new role and accelerate his career.

profiles.name: Nali
profiles.occupation: Data analyst at Capsumini, Bangalore (~4.5 LPA)
profiles.label: Hesitant Nali, Bangalore data analyst weighing MBA
profiles.description: Nali is from Shagaputnam and works as a data analyst at Capsumini in Bangalore, earning about 4.5 LPA. He has one year of experience building AI automation tools with Python, Databricks, and Power BI, but feels he lacks new skill exposure. He is cautious and price-sensitive, wondering whether to pursue an MBA or upskill some other way, and wants to grow his skills and improve his placement prospects. He seems hesitant, asking many questions about fees, free courses, and career moves.

profiles.name: Raj
profiles.occupation: Regional analyst at Altadex Cement, Odisha
profiles.label: Cautious Raj from Odisha, cement-company analyst
profiles.description: Raj is a regional analyst at Altadex Cement in Odisha with about 2.5 years of experience, transitioning from sales into an analytics role. He is price-sensitive and prefers an EMI payment plan, and he wants to move from intuition-based decisions to a more data-driven way of working. He appears focused and cautious, asking many details about the offering and fees.

profiles.name: Paulomi
profiles.occupation: Associate business analyst at Infosys (7.5 LPA)
profiles.label: Thoughtful Paulomi in Pune, Infosys business analyst
profiles.description: This is Paulomi from Kolkata, currently based in Pune. She's a 2023 computer engineering grad who started in cloud operations and is now an associate business analyst at Infosys on 7.5 LPA, sometimes stuck on the bench and actively looking to switch. She's engaged and thoughtful — the type to push back and ask probing questions — and has her eye on product-based companies and an eventual product owner role. She won't put money down on anything without checking with her dad first.

profiles.name: Shantakrishna
profiles.occupation: work-from-home video annotator at a data company (3 LPA)
profiles.label: Monosyllabic Shantakrishna, video annotator told to upskill
profiles.description: This is Shantakrishna, a 2021 B.Com grad from a town that came through garbled as 'Budkull'. After a year at a finance firm and two at a cooperative bank, he now works from home as a video annotator at a data company on 3 LPA, categorising shoppers by demographics. His employer told staff to upskill, and there's an internal data analyst opening he's eyeing, which is exactly why he's here. He's a man of very few words, answers in monosyllables, and tends to go along with whatever is suggested.

profiles.name: Sagar
profiles.occupation: UI/UX designer at a SaaS firm (4.2 LPA)
profiles.label: Chatty Sagar, UI/UX designer looking to future-proof himself
profiles.description: This is Sagar, a computer science grad with a six-month internship plus two years as a UI/UX designer at a firm building SaaS platforms, earning 4.2 LPA. He wants to understand clients' business models better and future-proof himself against AI. Extremely chatty, he launches into long monologues about AI versus human designers and critiques the Union Bank app's UX mid-call. He pushes back on small presentation details, asks pointed questions about tools and fees, and won't be hurried into decisions without time to think things over.

profiles.name: Ragini
profiles.occupation: senior consultant at Cognizant, business analyst on a Pearson project (18+ LPA)
profiles.label: Sharp Ragini from Rajasthan, Cognizant senior consultant
profiles.description: This is Ragini from Rajasthan, an engineering grad with around ten years of experience (about eight in IT), now a senior consultant at Cognizant on 18+ LPA working as a business analyst on a Pearson project. She sat tight through the layoff years and now wants to switch to a product company for a better package. She asks sharp, specific questions about curriculum selection, placement criteria, and whether it's manageable for a non-coder, and already tinkers with building AI agents herself. Cautious by nature, the type to go quiet and think things through before responding.

profiles.name: Nandini
profiles.occupation: business analyst at Conduent (4.5 LPA)
profiles.label: Methodical Nandini, business analyst who dislikes coding
profiles.description: This is Nandini Singh, a 2024 B.Tech computer science grad (8 CGPA) who was campus-placed as a business analyst at Conduent, where she has been for a year on 4.5 LPA after a six-month automation-testing internship at Freecharge. She dislikes coding and wants to upskill for predictive insights, aiming at lead or manager roles within five years. Methodical and inquisitive, she compared the IIM Ranchi and BITSOM options, asked what Masai actually contributes versus the institute, and probed placement timelines.

profiles.name: Chandan
profiles.occupation: business analyst working a 2-to-11 shift (8 LPA)
profiles.label: Confident Chandan from Hyderabad, night-shift business analyst
profiles.description: This is Chandan Sharma from Hyderabad, 26, an MBA in finance with five years' experience, the last three as a business analyst on 8 LPA, working a 2-to-11 shift. His work is repetitive and manual, so he wants to automate it, and he already leans on ChatGPT daily. Confident and observant, he cross-checks claims against the BITS Pilani website mid-call and asks about exams, doubt sessions and attendance for night-shift workers.

profiles.name: Manisha
profiles.occupation: business analyst at a US-based healthcare insurance firm (8.2 LPA)
profiles.label: Enthusiastic Manisha from Hyderabad, healthcare insurance analyst
profiles.description: This is Manisha from Hyderabad, a business analyst with 5+ years at a US-based healthcare insurance firm working on provider data, on a 2-to-11 pm shift out of Hi-Tech City. She is hands-on with SQL, Power BI, Tableau, advanced Excel and basic Python and holds an MBA in finance, but joined at 2.3 LPA and is only at 8.2 after five years in the same company, and knows she has missed out on hikes. Enthusiastic and cooperative, drawn by the Harvard/Wharton-name faculty, openly asking about highest packages and target roles, and promising to give 100%.

profiles.name: Ishika
profiles.occupation: management trainee at Nuziveedu Seeds, doing distributor and performance analysis
profiles.label: Agreeable Ishika in Hyderabad, seed-company management trainee
profiles.description: This is Ishika Singh, originally from Dehradun, now at the Hyderabad corporate office of Nuziveedu Seeds as a management trainee she joined in May. She has a master's in Agri Business Management from MANAGE Hyderabad and reports to the chief strategy office, doing distributor and performance analysis with Power BI and Excel. She's polite and agreeable on the call, asking practical questions about EMI timing, recordings and doubt sessions, though she's not one to rush into payments. She's drawn to a project-based, hands-on approach that will sharpen the analysis work she already does.

profiles.name: Shubhi
profiles.occupation: senior data analyst at Morningstar (8-9 LPA)
profiles.label: Composed Shubhi in Mumbai, Morningstar senior data analyst
profiles.description: This is Shubhi from Kota, Rajasthan, now living in Mumbai. She's a senior data analyst at Morningstar with nearly five years' experience earning 8-9 LPA, plus a postgrad diploma in finance from a commerce background. She's composed and methodical, asking about timings, fees, exams, pandas and the campus immersion. She feels she's lagging on technical skills that would push her career forward and wants to close that gap.

profiles.name: Ashwini
profiles.occupation: client-side team manager on a ServiceNow project, ~10 years' experience (8 in business analytics)
profiles.label: Skeptical Ashwini in Bangalore, ten-year analytics veteran
profiles.description: This is Ashwini from Nagpur, working in Bangalore with around 10 years' experience, eight of them in business analytics. She manages a team client-side on a ServiceNow project that's already rolling out AI agents, and is keen to grow despite having no coding background. Very sharp and skeptical on the call, she probes how much of the content is actually covered, questions the 3.5-vs-7 CGPA logic, insists on live trainer interaction over recordings, and squeezes the session between back-to-back meetings. Refuses to be pushed into same-day decisions and wants time to compare options properly.

profiles.name: Amit
profiles.occupation: quality analyst in compliance auditing at Amazon (hybrid), ~9 years' experience
profiles.label: Stalling Amit from Pune, Amazon quality analyst
profiles.description: This is Amit from Pune, currently working hybrid at Amazon as a quality analyst in compliance auditing with around 9 years of experience, 7 of them at Amazon. His work already involves data analysis and RCA, and he dabbles in AI but has no formal credentials, which he feels blocks better opportunities. On the call he is polite but a serious staller, the type to keep saying he needs time and to want his brother's word before spending anything.

profiles.name: Dhanush
profiles.occupation: application developer at NTT
profiles.label: Reserved Dhanush from Karnataka, NTT application developer
profiles.description: This is Dhanush from Karnataka, working as an application developer at NTT with around 3 years of experience building websites and apps, and he knows the Dart language. He hasn't done any research beforehand. He is very quiet and reserved on the call — answers in single words and raises almost no questions beyond the exam format and payment deadline. He likes to research an institute himself before making any decision.

profiles.name: Mehul
profiles.occupation: assistant manager in growth (recently joined a new company)
profiles.label: Detail-oriented Mehul, ex-Flipkart growth assistant manager
profiles.description: This is Mehul, a B.Tech grad with two years of experience. He joined Flipkart through campus placement, worked there two years, and switched to a new company about three weeks ago where he's an assistant manager in growth, working heavily on analytics. He came across it on Instagram and wants to speed up and automate the work he already does. He's detail-oriented and price-aware — quick to cross-check quoted fees against the website — and wants to clear any payment with his parents first.

profiles.name: Krutik
profiles.occupation: business analyst and EA to the CEO at Apollo Hospitals
profiles.label: 25 year old cooperative Krutik, Apollo hospitals analyst
profiles.description: This is Krutik Raval, 25, from Mumbai but working in Pune as a business analyst and EA to the CEO at Apollo Hospitals. He has a bachelor's in hospital administration and an MBA in hospital & healthcare management (2025), briefly worked as a management trainee at a Nairobi hospital before returning to India for medical treatment, and has about 1.5 years of experience. He wants to close the gaps in his daily hospital operations work. He's polite and cooperative, but his employer is footing the bill, so he won't pay until he gets reimbursement approval from his CEO.

profiles.name: Kiran
profiles.occupation: IT support engineer (around 8 LPA)
profiles.label: Rambly Kiran from Bangalore, stalled IT support engineer
profiles.description: This is Kiran from Bangalore. He's an IT support engineer with about 5.3 years of experience earning around 8 LPA, having started out in BPO, and he did his BCom through correspondence while working. He's hesitant and rambly on the call, admits he isn't technically strong, agrees with everything the counsellor says, but does probe about placements and fishes for a discount. He wants to switch careers because his IT support career has stalled and he can't clear technical interviews.

profiles.name: Pavan
profiles.occupation: IT job
profiles.label: Quiet Pavan from Bhopal, agreeable IT worker
profiles.description: This is Pavan, a BTech grad from Oriental College of Technology in Bhopal who now works an IT job. He came across it on social media and admits he hasn't researched it at all. He comes across as quiet and agreeable, without much else giving his personality away.

profiles.name: Chandana
profiles.occupation: VLSI layout engineer at Accenture
profiles.label: Enthusiastic Chandana from Bangalore, VLSI layout engineer
profiles.description: This is Chandana (name garbled) from Bangalore, a VLSI layout engineer at Accenture with 10+ years in semiconductor design and a BE in electronics and telecommunication. She's not chasing a job switch or a startup — she's after personal growth in her current role, where so far she only uses ChatGPT for emails and such. She's enthusiastic and agreeable, and asks practical questions like group-versus-solo projects and how Wednesday classes fit her 9-to-7 job.

profiles.name: Ravindar
profiles.occupation: driver
profiles.label: Hesitant Ravindar, driver joining from the road
profiles.description: Ravindar is currently traveling, likely driving, and joining the call from a moving vehicle. He appears hesitant, repeatedly checking his internet connection before proceeding. He works as a driver, though no salary or employer details were mentioned. He is interested in the call and wants to learn more about it.

profiles.name: Saran
profiles.occupation: runs his own business
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Saran Saker, Tamil Nadu business owner
profiles.description: Saran Saker is a 2020 graduate from Tamil Nadu who runs his own business. He is currently working in a non-technical field and wants to automate his business processes. He is price-sensitive, asks many logistical questions, and prefers weekend classes. He sees this as a way to up-skill for his own venture rather than a career change.

profiles.name: Shivramaya
profiles.occupation: BPO claims processor
profiles.label: Hesitant Shivramaya from Hyderabad, BPO claims processor
profiles.description: Shivramaya is from Yanam Puticherry but currently lives in Hyderabad. She has four years of experience in a BPO handling claims processing, a non-technical role. She is cautious about costs and unsure about the fee structure, but wants to transition into a better career. On the call she sounds hesitant and price-sensitive, asking many clarification questions.

profiles.name: Sonu
profiles.occupation: assistant manager leading a team of 8-10 in fund operations at a global bank
profiles.label: Chatty Sonu from Delhi, bank assistant manager
profiles.description: Sonu is a finance professional based in Delhi, currently an assistant manager leading a team of 8-10 in fund operations at a global bank. He holds a master's degree from University of Calcutta (2018) and has extensive experience in trade processing, client onboarding, and reconciliation. He appears confident and chatty, eager to automate tasks and stay relevant in his current role. He wants to support better decision-making and automation in his finance work.

profiles.name: Yogesh
profiles.occupation: part-time grocery shop worker in the UK
profiles.education: electronics and communication graduate, Hyderabad
profiles.label: Indecisive Yogesh, grocery-shop worker in the UK
profiles.description: Yogesh is a recent electronic and communication graduate from Hyderabad who is now working part-time in a grocery shop in the UK. He is hesitant and indecisive, repeatedly asking about timing, fees, and batch schedules while trying to balance his shifts. He wants better career prospects while staying in the UK long-term.

profiles.name: Ashwani
profiles.occupation: finance and marketing at Accenture
profiles.education: recent MBA graduate
profiles.label: Cautious Ashwani from Ghaziabad, Accenture MBA grad
profiles.description: Ashwani is a recent MBA graduate working in finance and marketing at Accenture in Ghaziabad. He has about a year of experience and is looking to switch careers for better growth. He appears cautious and detail-oriented, repeatedly checking fee and schedule details. He wants to boost his career prospects.

profiles.name: Shahnawaz
profiles.occupation: runs his own land development business
profiles.education: 2019 Electronics & Telecom grad, College of Engineering Roorkee
profiles.label: Practical Shahnawaz from Roorkee, land-development businessman
profiles.description: This is Shahnawaz from Roorkee, a 2019 Electronics & Telecom grad from College of Engineering Roorkee. He runs his own land development business — buying land, getting approvals, laying roads and utilities, plotting and selling — which he started himself. He prefers Hindi, is polite and practical, and mainly wants to know how this could help him predict prices and find buyers for his projects. He likes to handle things in his own time rather than being rushed.

profiles.name: Shalin
profiles.occupation: merchant navy sailor
profiles.education: diploma in nautical science
profiles.label: Hesitant Shalin from Jammu, merchant navy sailor
profiles.description: This is Shalin from Jammu & Kashmir. He's done a diploma in nautical science and is currently sailing (merchant navy side), looking to explore other career options. He comes in completely cold — first time on this call, zero research — and gives short, hesitant answers.

profiles.name: Shlok
profiles.occupation: Key Account Manager at Zomato
profiles.education: BFM degree
profiles.label: Blunt Shlok from Mumbai, Zomato key account manager
profiles.description: This is Shlok from Kalyan, Mumbai. He's a Key Account Manager at Zomato — 1.5 years full-time after interning there during his BFM degree — managing a portfolio of around 300 restaurant partners. He's an independent decision-maker and a tough customer: he grills people on which companies actually show up at placement, salary ranges, and how the Wednesday class timing would clash with office plus a two-hour commute. Hard to convince and not shy about saying so.

profiles.name: Geetanjali
profiles.occupation: government sector staffer in the education department
profiles.label: Geetanjali, education-department staffer on a patchy phone line
profiles.description: This is Geetanjali, who works in the government sector in the education department. That's about all that's known of her background, and her motivation is unclear. She joins from a phone, with a patchy camera and audio setup.

profiles.name: Aniket
profiles.occupation: solar module design R&D engineer at a renewable energy company
profiles.education: 2020 mechanical engineering grad
profiles.label: Polite Aniket, solar R&D engineer wanting placement surety
profiles.description: This is Aniket Sharma, a 2020 mechanical engineering grad who's spent nearly three years at a renewable energy company doing solar module design in the R&D team, earning 7.5 LPA. He wants to switch careers for growth and already touches Excel, SQL and Power BI in his job. He's a polite, yes-sir type who mostly listens and rarely pushes back. Placement support is his single biggest ask — he'll ask point blank if there's surety of placement.

profiles.name: Shilpi
profiles.occupation: postgraduate commerce teacher at a DAV school
profiles.label: Cautious Shilpi from Ranchi, school commerce teacher
profiles.description: This is Shilpi Sharma, originally from Ranchi, now living in another town where she's a postgraduate commerce teacher at a DAV school. She has five years of teaching experience, earns 30k a month, also takes online public-speaking classes for Planet Spark, and is a mother to a seven-year-old. She feels her skills need updating and wants to grow her career. Earnest and interested, but cautious — she needs at least a day to consult her husband before paying for anything.

profiles.name: Anirudha
profiles.occupation: marketing job at a private steel company
profiles.education: B.Tech Civil grad, around 2025
profiles.label: Patient Anirudha, steel-company marketer on shaky network
profiles.description: This is Anirudha, a B.Tech Civil grad who now works a full-time marketing job at a private steel company. He graduated around 2025. He joins on a shaky network with frequent audio drops, but stays patient and polite through all the disruptions.

profiles.name: Amit
profiles.occupation: non-tech operational risk at Wells Fargo
profiles.label: 38 year old probing Amit, Wells Fargo veteran
profiles.description: This is Amit, 38, originally from Ranchi and now living in Whitefield, Bangalore. He has 14 years at Wells Fargo in non-tech operational risk and his bank just gave him enterprise Copilot access. He is clear he does not care about certificates or placement — he wants practical skills he can actually apply in his current job. Thoughtful but probing: he wonders whether he could attend classes while delaying the balance payment, and worries it might be too tech-heavy or too shallow for what he needs.

profiles.name: Ankit
profiles.occupation: runs his father's electrical trading business, Lakshmi Traders (wires, switchgears, MCBs)
profiles.label: Candid Ankit, running family electrical trading business
profiles.description: This is Ankit, who dropped out of B.Com in second year and has spent about eight-nine years running his father's electrical trading business, Lakshmi Traders (wires, switchgears, MCBs), handling everything from sales to purchase. The business has struggled for the last six-seven months, so after nine years away from studies he wants to upskill, partly to apply what he learns to the business, partly to take a placement if something better comes along. Humble and candid, but money is tight for him and arranging a fee at short notice would be hard.

profiles.name: Sujan
profiles.occupation: key account manager at Zomato doing B2B sales (6.6 LPA plus 15% variable)
profiles.label: Decisive Sujan from Visakhapatnam, Zomato account manager
profiles.description: This is Sujan from Visakhapatnam. He is a key account manager at Zomato doing B2B sales, about one and a half years in after starting as an acquisition manager, earning 6.6 LPA plus 15% variable. He is tired of being the client-facing 'face of Zomato' and wants to move into a less client-facing role. Well-prepared, he had already read up the duration, tools and schedule, and is torn between this and a product management course, and he's a careful negotiator on EMI terms.

profiles.name: Nadeem
profiles.occupation: business development executive (company name garbled, sounds like Flipkart), works 10-to-7
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Nadeem from Bangalore, business development executive
profiles.description: This is Mohammed Nadeem, originally from Kerala but raised and settled in Bangalore with his family. He works 10-to-7 as a business development executive (company name garbled, sounds like Flipkart) and wants both to upskill and to accelerate a career shift. Very price-sensitive, he can only manage 4-5K a month and finds 7-8K EMIs out of the question, and he cannot start in June due to a commitment. Polite but evasive: he has not researched anything and tends to keep asking for more time rather than giving a straight answer.

profiles.name: Ashutosh
profiles.occupation: business development veteran at Eversub India (Subway's master franchise), runs retail and network expansion for the North region
profiles.label: Detail-hungry Ashutosh from Noida, Subway franchise veteran
profiles.description: This is Ashutosh, a business development veteran with roughly 14 years across Educomp, Carnival Cinemas, Domino's and now Eversub India (Subway's master franchise), where he runs retail and network expansion for the North region. He lives in Noida, commutes to a Gurgaon office, manages a team of five and travels two days a week, so weekday class timings worry him. He is completely non-technical beyond casual ChatGPT use and finds the fee structure unclear. Engaged but detail-hungry, he keeps pressing for the full curriculum, phases and projects.

profiles.name: Nishant
profiles.occupation: senior design engineer at Tata Elxsi, 5+ years in mechanical design
profiles.label: Rigorous Nishant from Bangalore, Tata Elxsi engineer
profiles.description: This is Nishant Shetty from Bangalore, originally from Kundapura in Udupi, a senior design engineer at Tata Elxsi with 5+ years in mechanical design — vehicle benchmarking, electric tractors, and now Jaguar Land Rover engines. He wants to move his engineering judgement into a more analytical role. Easily the most rigorous questioner of the lot: batch history, tech versus non-tech placement ratios, whether the curriculum keeps pace, individual versus group projects, alumni access. He likes to compare options with 'quantitative numbers' and won't be pushed into on-the-spot decisions.

profiles.name: Anu
profiles.occupation: operations manager at TCS in finance, accounts and audit (12-15 LPA)
profiles.label: 39 year old thorough Anu, TCS operations manager
profiles.description: This is Anu, an operations manager at TCS with about 15 years in finance, accounts and audit, earning 12-15 LPA. She's 39, a semi-qualified CA with a postgrad in commerce, managing a team of 10 under managers who report to the CFO. On the call she's polite but extremely thorough, with pointed questions about GST, EMI terms, the exact Excel syllabus and even referral discounts, and she likes to take time and discuss with people before committing to anything. She wants to cut work pressure, land an overdue senior manager promotion, and eventually freelance with her own clients.

profiles.name: Sunil
profiles.occupation: delivery manager in US IT recruitment (12-15 LPA)
profiles.label: Chatty Sunil from Haryana, recruitment delivery manager
profiles.description: This is Sunil from Haryana, currently around Noida. He's a 2015 mechanical B.Tech who drifted into US IT recruitment and is now a delivery manager with 5-7 years' experience on roughly 12-15 LPA. He's chatty and probing, grilling the counsellor on placements, switching into fintech, EMI interest rates, and citing his cousin's pay-after-placement Masai course. He wants a hedge because he expects recruitment work to decline in a couple of years, and placement assistance is his stated main concern.

profiles.name: Rana
profiles.occupation: operations head for the last 18 years
profiles.label: Calm Kaushik, 18-year operations head on patchy network
profiles.description: This is Rana Kaushik, who introduces himself as an operations head for the last 18 years. He joins on a patchy network that keeps cutting out, and stays calm and cooperative through the audio trouble, patiently confirming whether he can be heard. Beyond his intro he gives away very little about himself or what drew him to the call.

profiles.name: Arzoo
profiles.occupation: sales head at Meds Talent in US healthcare staffing (20 LPA)
profiles.label: 30 year old confident Arzoo, staffing sales head
profiles.description: This is Arzoo, 30, a B.Com grad from Rajasthan now settled in Delhi NCR. She has nine years in US healthcare staffing and is sales head at Meds Talent on 20 LPA, after earlier delivery and associate director stints. She's confident and articulate, takes the call from office through constant connection drops, and engages enthusiastically rather than haggling on price. She's explicitly not after a career switch — she wants to stay ahead of the shift she sees sweeping the staffing industry.

profiles.name: Lokesh
profiles.occupation: validation analyst at Biocon (5.5 LPA), three years' experience
profiles.label: 28 year old earnest Lokesh, Biocon validation analyst
profiles.description: This is Lokesh from Rourkela, Odisha, 28, working in Bangalore as a validation analyst at Biocon on 5.5 LPA with three years' experience. He's an MSc Biotechnology grad who's been trying to change his career direction for six months and is also prepping CAT for a pharma-focused MBA. He's earnest and engaged, asking sensible questions about class timings, tools and EMI amounts, but he wilts when put under pressure. He wants to move toward business development roles within pharma.

profiles.name: Rakesh
profiles.occupation: insurance area manager at Chola MS General Insurance
profiles.label: Cautious Rakesh from Assam, insurance area manager
profiles.description: This is Rakesh from Assam (Silchar, by the sound of it), an area manager at Chola MS General Insurance with about 15 years in the industry. He runs a nine-person team covering the Northeast - Assam, Mizoram, Manipur - and admits he struggles to read all the MIS reports that land on him. He's a busy, cautious buyer: takes an office call mid-session, concedes he hasn't read the details yet, and insists on verifying everything himself before deciding anything. He wants to boost his career and to back the business he vaguely plans to start someday.

profiles.name: Sinha
profiles.occupation: chartered accountant at Deloitte (transfer pricing and international taxation)
profiles.education: B.Com from Lucknow University
profiles.label: Articulate Deloitte chartered accountant from Lucknow
profiles.description: This is a chartered accountant from Lucknow (email surname Sinha; the first name never comes through clearly). They qualified in May 2024, did six months at PwC, and have been at Deloitte since February 2025 in transfer pricing and international taxation, with a B.Com from Lucknow University. Articulate and analytical on the call - asks pointed questions about how this helps a domain switch and exactly what unlocks placements. Money decisions go through their mother, and they wouldn't spend an amount like this without discussing it with her first.

profiles.name: Sandeep
profiles.occupation: senior manager in accounts and finance
profiles.label: Terse Sandeep, senior finance manager, personal growth only
profiles.description: This is Sandeep, a senior manager in accounts and finance with about 15 years of experience, B.Com plus MBA. He is looking purely for personal growth, not a job switch. He takes calls from his office where he is hard to pin down - his answers come as short one-liners, and he is only properly free to talk after 6 pm.

profiles.name: Shubham
profiles.occupation: vocational trainer on a government school project
profiles.label: Non-committal Shubham, Hindi-preferring vocational trainer
profiles.description: This is Shubham, a B.E. in IT who briefly ran a company with friends, then moved back to his home town and now works as a vocational trainer on a government school project. He wants to build new skills because he sees future demand in IT. He is more comfortable in Hindi, stays vague whenever budget comes up, and leans on a non-committal 'I'll think about it' instead of giving straight answers.

profiles.name: Pushpendra
profiles.occupation: corporate relationship manager doing after-sales support at an IT firm selling GST software
profiles.label: Slippery Pushpendra from Ahmedabad, GST-software relationship manager
profiles.description: This is Pushpendra from Ahmedabad, an M.Com who works as a corporate relationship manager doing after-sales support at an IT firm selling GST software, 5 years of experience on 5 LPA. He applied off an Instagram ad without checking any details and admits he is 'just exploring' for now. He is polite but slippery - the type to want to talk to his parents and ask for one or two days rather than give a straight yes or no.

profiles.name: Jashwant
profiles.occupation: works at Unicorn Info Solutions (unspecified role, 8 years)
profiles.label: Evasive Jashwant, calling mid-ride from the highway
profiles.description: This is Jashwant, who has worked at Unicorn Info Solutions for the past 8 years in an unspecified role. He is a 12th pass (2014) who started a B.Com but dropped out after bad marks in second year because he couldn't manage work and studies. He joins the call while riding home on the highway on borrowed Wi-Fi. He sounds agreeable and asks about placements, but turns evasive the moment money comes up.

profiles.name: Nirmal
profiles.occupation: ground transportation support at Yatra (corporate cab bookings)
profiles.label: Chatty Nirmal, non-technical cab-operations guy at Yatra
profiles.description: This is Nirmal, who handles ground transportation support at Yatra - assigning corporate cab bookings to vendors - with about 10 years in the car rental industry. He calls himself an 'absolutely non-technical guy' and repeatedly worries whether he can cope, hoping to switch internally to Yatra's technical department. He found out about this via an Instagram ad and joins from office since his shift is 24x7 rotational. He is chatty and curious on the call.

profiles.name: Dhruv
profiles.occupation: works in his father's wholesale-retail medicine business
profiles.label: 20 year old deferential Dhruv from Patiala
profiles.description: This is Dhruv, a 20-year-old from Patiala, Punjab. He is 12th pass, took a two-year drop, and works in his father's wholesale-retail medicine business, which he wants to digitalize and grow. He is polite and deferential on the call but knows almost nothing technical, and admits he may struggle to attend classes because his father may not spare him the time. He won't spend money without asking his parents first.

profiles.name: Manu
profiles.occupation: warehouse manager at Access Warehousing (logistics and supply chain)
profiles.label: Agreeable Manu from Bangalore, warehouse manager
profiles.description: This is Manu, a warehouse manager at Access Warehousing, a logistics and supply chain firm in Bangalore. He is originally from West Bengal but has been in Bangalore for 16-17 years, with 15 years of work experience. He is looking for career growth, says he genuinely enjoys analysis, and can spare 8-10 hours a week. He is agreeable with short answers, though any money decision goes through his family first.

profiles.name: Ayush
profiles.occupation: engineer at Maruti (around 12.5 LPA)
profiles.education: diploma plus a degree in electronics and communication
profiles.label: 24 year old confident Ayush, Maruti engineer
profiles.description: This is Ayush, 24, originally from Vaishali, Bihar, now in Gurgaon. He did a diploma, then a degree in electronics and communication while working, and has been an engineer at Maruti for 5 years (second company after a Japanese firm), earning around 12.5 LPA. He worked on new engine production benchmarking, logging torque and output data in Excel, and eventually wants to do an MBA. He is confident and articulate, asks pointed questions about placement criteria, and is cautious about fees.

profiles.name: Afzal
profiles.occupation: Area business manager in pharma sales (anti-depressants, anti-epileptics, pain management)
profiles.label: Cautious Afzal from Bareilly, pharma sales manager
profiles.description: This is Afzal from Bareilly, UP, working in Delhi as an area business manager in pharma sales — anti-depressants, anti-epileptics and pain management — with 8 years of experience and roughly 10-12 LPA. He has an MSc in Organic Chemistry, did a business analyst certification three years ago that never led to a switch, and has no programming knowledge. He wants to change fields and asks measured, practical questions: is it useful for someone in pharma, who would back it, EMI interest, placement odds. He is cautious by nature, preferring to take the details away and think things over rather than decide on the spot.

profiles.name: Sitesh
profiles.occupation: Quality engineer at Hapid Enterprises (an MSME making suspension bushes)
profiles.label: Fed-up Sitesh, overworked quality engineer wanting out
profiles.description: This is Sitesh, a 2024 B.Tech mechanical (automobile) graduate now working as a quality engineer at Hapid Enterprises, an MSME making suspension bushes. He is fed up — duty crept from 8 to 12 hours plus Sundays, no growth, and his tier-1 company peers out-earn him — so he wants out of mechanical, inspired by a relative working at ITC. He knows some Python and SQL from school and asks sharp questions, including why he should pick this over other options. He is engaged but careful, describes himself as 'just exploring', and wouldn't spend without talking to his parents first.

profiles.name: Apurva
profiles.occupation: Pilates teacher (works on-site with clients and physiotherapists)
profiles.education: Distance MBA in marketing management, four semesters done with two pending
profiles.label: Blunt Apurva from Mumbai, Pilates teacher
profiles.description: This is Apurva from Mumbai, a Pilates teacher who works on-site with clients and physiotherapists, and is partway through a distance MBA in marketing management (four semesters done, two pending). She wants to try something new with flexibility and work-from-home potential, but has no concrete plan yet. She came across it through an Instagram ad and knew nothing about the details. She is blunt and combative — she expects whoever she is talking to to have read the CV she submitted, and will question the whole process if they haven't.

profiles.name: Mohammed
profiles.occupation: Customer relationship manager at a forex company (sales, consulting, field work)
profiles.label: Earnest Zaid from Amravati, forex relationship manager
profiles.description: This is Mohammed Zaid from Amravati, Maharashtra, a BCA graduate with a fresh MBA in marketing and business analytics. He has been a customer relationship manager at a forex company for about 13 months, doing sales, consulting and field work. He self-taught SQL, Power BI, Tableau and advanced Excel from YouTube but rates himself only 5-6 out of 10 since he has never done real projects; he is looking to grow into a stronger role now and into brand or product management later. He is earnest and engaged and uses ChatGPT, Gemini and Claude daily, but he won't pay for anything before checking with his brother, who works in an MNC.

profiles.name: Maruti
profiles.occupation: Self-employed since 2018 across construction, properties and investments; currently working in FMCG
profiles.label: Worried Maruti, self-employed commerce guy in FMCG
profiles.description: This is Maruti, a commerce guy — B.Com from Nanded and a PGDM in finance from MIT World Peace University, Pune. Self-employed since 2018 across construction, properties and investments, and currently working in FMCG. He was drawn in by the pay-after-placement angle, since finance is his background. He is earnest but worried, asking whether he can handle the technical, hands-on side with zero coding background.

profiles.name: Shoaib
profiles.occupation: Associate executive at a non-IT company (about 3 years of experience)
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Shoaib, associate executive awaiting payday
profiles.description: This is Shoaib, an associate executive at a non-IT company with about 3 years of experience, though he holds a B.E. in Computer Science (graduated around 2021). He wants to move into a more in-demand role for career growth. He is quite price-sensitive — he picked the 2-3k per month budget option in the application, asks for exact EMI amounts and due dates, and money stays tight until his salary lands on the 5th. Otherwise an agreeable, low-friction prospect who asks practical questions about placements, doubt sessions and reassessment.

profiles.name: Guru
profiles.occupation: Digital marketing strategist; heads the strategy vertical at Lixel and Flamingo, an agency in Gurgaon (300+ brands like Boat, Biba, Libas)
profiles.label: Skeptical Guru Prasad, Gurgaon digital marketing strategist
profiles.description: This is Guru Prasad, a digital marketing strategist with 4-5 years of experience who heads the strategy vertical at Lixel and Flamingo, an agency in Gurgaon, handling 300+ brands like Boat, Biba and Libas. He already uses Claude and ChatGPT heavily and has built his own prompt-driven analysis tools and chatbots. He is confident, articulate and openly skeptical — he keeps challenging what extra could actually be taught to someone like him, and he is weighing his options.

profiles.name: Sudhir
profiles.occupation: Runs his own competitive-exams coaching institute (~100 students), teaching subjects like geography to SSC, bank, railway and police aspirants
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Sudhir, runs competitive-exam coaching institute
profiles.description: This is Sudhir, who did his MCA back in 2012 and now runs his own competitive-exams coaching institute with around 100 students, teaching subjects like geography to SSC, bank, railway and police aspirants. He wants to sharpen his profile with future-ready skills and maybe land work with good companies. He is inquisitive but quite price-sensitive — calls the 52,000+GST fee expensive and asks about scholarships, demo classes and EMI amounts. He is also firm that how much time he takes to decide is entirely up to him.

profiles.name: Reshma
profiles.occupation: Senior analyst at Accenture (13 years) on the corporate finance team, handling customer and contract setup with SAP and Copilot
profiles.label: Indecisive Reshma from Bangalore, 13-year Accenture analyst
profiles.description: This is Reshma from Bangalore, a senior analyst at Accenture with 13 years there — her first and only company — on the corporate finance team handling customer and contract setup, working with SAP and Copilot daily. She feels stagnated and underpaid versus the market and wants to upskill. On the call she is soft-spoken, cautious and indecisive — she has nobody to consult but simply cannot make a quick decision, typically needing at least a day to mull anything over.

profiles.name: Neha
profiles.occupation: Runs her own customized sustainable garment export business (US and UK clients) and a D2C brand, Satvik Style, on Shopify
profiles.label: 41 year old chatty Neha, garment-export entrepreneur
profiles.description: This is Neha, 41, from Jaipur — BSc in biotechnology plus an MBA in HR and marketing. She started as a business manager at Sierra Living Concepts, then built her own customized sustainable garment export business from 2017 with US and UK clients, and in 2025 launched a D2C brand called Satvik Style on Shopify. She wants to sharpen demand forecasting and inventory in her own business, not land a job. She is warm, chatty and genuinely enthusiastic, and mentions needing to discuss bigger decisions with her husband.

profiles.name: Kiran
profiles.occupation: consultant at the National Medical Commission
profiles.label: Stuck Kiran from Almora, medical-commission consultant
profiles.description: This is Kiran, calling from her hometown Almora in Uttarakhand. She has a BBA and has spent three years as a consultant at the administrative headquarters of the National Medical Commission, a statutory body in Dwarka — very traditional work centred on coordination, workflow management and HR. She sees no growth there and wants to stay future-relevant, though she admits she hasn't planned whether she wants a career switch or just an upgrade.

profiles.name: Vineeth
profiles.occupation: team leader in sales operations at Markets and Markets, a research firm
profiles.label: Methodical Vineeth from Pune, scam-wary sales leader
profiles.description: This is Vineeth, 35, from Pune — an MBA in marketing (2015-17) with 9 years of experience, four as a sales executive and now a team leader in sales operations at Markets and Markets, a research firm. He lives in sales data already, automates Excel sheets, uses NotebookLM, and has done self-paced Excel and SAP courses. He is a sharp, methodical buyer — asks about ROI from previous batches, placement numbers, attendance rules and the Masai-BITSOM relationship — and is scam-wary because of a fraud in his family. He sees this as an upgrade towards promotion, not a career change.

profiles.name: Pavan
profiles.occupation: junior engineer on a government university construction project
profiles.label: Idealistic Pavan from Hyderabad, government-project junior engineer
profiles.description: This is likely Pavan (heard as 'Avan'), 26, from Hyderabad, a 2022 civil engineering graduate. He spent about three years in the family's real estate business doing documentation and land procurement, and is now a junior engineer on a government university construction project in Khammam, Telangana, earning just 16,000 a month. He's chatty and idealistic, rambling about serving society through infrastructure, and admits he has zero coding knowledge. He sees this as a whole package to upskill and switch into a new career.

profiles.name: Ashish
profiles.occupation: real estate salesman who also helps run the family solar plant business
profiles.label: Slippery Ashish from Jaipur, overworked real-estate salesman
profiles.description: This is Ashish from Jaipur, who joined a real estate sales job about a month ago and also helps run the family's solar plant business. His hours are brutal, anywhere from 4 to 13-14 a day with travel, so he doubts he can commit to classes. He's polite but slippery, the type to keep saying he'll think about it and let you know. His motivation is vague: gaining knowledge is never wrong and it might help his business ideas.

profiles.name: Vaishnavi
profiles.occupation: modular kitchen designer and sales coordinator in interiors
profiles.education: BBA, second semester (alongside diplomas in mechanical engineering and interior design)
profiles.label: Tough Vaishnavi from Pune, modular kitchen designer
profiles.description: This is Vaishnavi from Pune, a modular kitchen designer and sales coordinator with 6+ years in interiors (diplomas in mechanical engineering and interior design), now also doing a BBA in her second semester. She earns about 6.5 LPA fixed, 7.5-8 with variables, paid in cash, which she can't show on paper. She's a tough, research-heavy prospect who grills on batch size, mentors, placement guarantees and discounts, and compares everything against Simplilearn. She wants to switch to a corporate role for work-life balance and financial growth.

profiles.name: Srinivas
profiles.occupation: morning-shift operations worker at Star Tech, verifying criminal records for a US company
profiles.label: Earnest Srinivas from Chittoor, on-shift operations worker
profiles.description: This is Srinivas from Palamaner in Chittoor district, Andhra Pradesh, a B.Com grad who passed out in 2026 a year late because of a backlog. He works morning shifts at Star Tech, a third-party for a US company verifying criminal records, basically operations work, and wants a job change expecting 6+ LPA. He's earnest and agreeable, says he's comfortable with the fee and EMI, but is firm that he must talk to his parents before committing to anything.

profiles.name: Priyanka
profiles.occupation: assistant manager in industrial engineering and production planning at Reliance Retail's garment business
profiles.label: Terse Priyanka in Jaipur, Reliance Retail assistant manager
profiles.description: This is Priyanka Jaiswal from Gorakhpur, UP, working in Jaipur as an assistant manager in industrial engineering and production planning at Reliance Retail's garment business, 9 years in the same industry, earning 8 LPA. She's spooked by AI-driven layoffs and recession in her field and wants to switch careers, targeting roughly double, around 15 LPA, with a longer-term plan for an online grocery business. She gives terse answers and is firmly price-sensitive — fees in this range feel very high to her. She's unmarried and makes her own money decisions.

profiles.name: Dhiraj
profiles.occupation: assistant manager in sales/growth and partnerships at FMCG startup Khetika
profiles.education: MBA in marketing via IGNOU correspondence (B.Com grad)
profiles.label: Fee-blocked Dhiraj from Noida, FMCG startup manager
profiles.description: This is Dhiraj from Noida Sector 5, an assistant manager in sales/growth and partnerships at FMCG startup Khetika after two years at Farmley, about three years' experience total, earning 7.2 LPA after a fresh appraisal. He's a B.Com grad finishing an MBA in marketing via IGNOU correspondence, with exams starting June 1. He already overviews P&L and category data and wants to upskill so he can work more efficiently, but is upfront that the 52k fee, not the time, is the major blocker.

profiles.name: Mayur
profiles.occupation: procurement and purchase professional at a private firm handling plant machinery and packing material
profiles.label: Quiet Mayur from Mumbai, stuck procurement professional
profiles.description: This is Mayur from Mumbai. He's done BE Mechanical plus an MBA in Operations and has five years in procurement and purchase — currently at a private firm handling plant machinery and packing material, earlier at Alok Industries (part of Reliance) managing annual maintenance contracts — earning 8 LPA. He feels procurement has no career growth or salary headroom and wants to switch careers. He's a quiet, agreeable listener who gives short answers, and the type who needs to discuss with someone before any payment decision.

profiles.name: Kaushal
profiles.occupation: tech sales at Progress Software
profiles.label: Skeptical Kaushal, tech-sales grad wanting a switch
profiles.description: This is Kaushal, a BTech CS grad from PES University (2025, 6.75 CGPA) now working in tech sales at Progress Software. He didn't focus enough in college to land a dev role, leaned on his communication strength, and now wants to switch back into a technical role. He's confident and articulate — plays drums and chess — and asks pointed questions about placement statistics, assurances and juggling study with a full-time job. He's openly skeptical of the brand and not one to decide on the spot.

profiles.name: Mamata
profiles.occupation: sales associate (edtech, BFSI, real estate)
profiles.label: 40 year old firm Mamata, ex-teacher in sales
