profiles.description: This is Mamata, 40 years old, with a BE in Electronics and Communication. She spent around four years as a science HOD/school teacher and then worked as a sales associate across edtech, BFSI and real estate — 10+ years total in non-technical roles. She believes she's good at analysing data and wants to shift into a new role for the career opportunities; she came across the offering on Facebook. She's calm but firm under pressure, and any spending decision has to be discussed with her husband.

profiles.name: Prabolika
profiles.occupation: safety manager in flight safety (aviation)
profiles.label: Curt Prabolika, flight-safety manager wanting out of aviation
profiles.description: This is Prabolika, an aviation professional with 6+ years of experience, currently a safety manager in flight safety, with about a year as a technical writer before that. She wants out of aviation for better pay and remote-work options, since her current role demands physical presence six days a week. She's curt and a bit defensive — pushes back with 'why can't I change my career?' and admits she has no big attachment to the institute, she's just exploring after seeing it on Instagram.

profiles.name: Ajit
profiles.occupation: B2B operations at Bajaj Finance (loan documentation)
profiles.label: Pragmatic Ajit from Doda, Bajaj Finance B2B operations
profiles.description: This is Ajit from Doda, Jammu & Kashmir. He holds a Masters in Economics and has spent three years in B2B operations at Bajaj Finance, processing and cross-checking loan documentation. He's bluntly worried his job is disappearing — he reckons 80% of his operations work is already automated — and wants to upskill before it vanishes, though he has no idea yet which roles to target. He's serious and pragmatic with brief answers, and would need to go arrange funds before paying any booking amount.

profiles.name: Manoj
profiles.occupation: manager in the Indian Navy (25 years technical service, retiring next year)
profiles.label: Disciplined Manoj, Navy manager retiring next year
profiles.description: This is Manoj, a manager in the Indian Navy with 25 years of technical service, retiring next year. He found it through an Instagram reel and registered immediately because of the BITS name, which he already knew. He's polite and disciplined but pushes back firmly on the six-month duration — he'd prefer something shorter and worries his heavy service commitments will clash with the class timings. He wants the skills to set up a second career after retirement, with options like radiation safety on nuclear platforms or the Merchant Navy.

profiles.name: Sudipta
profiles.occupation: senior operations officer at Amul (dairy industry)
profiles.label: Matter-of-fact Sudipta, Amul dairy operations officer
profiles.description: This is Sudipta, a senior officer at Amul with 8+ years in the dairy industry — the last four at Amul and earlier at Mother Dairy — and a BTech in dairy/food technology. He looks after operations and management: inventory, quality measurements and processing parameters before dispatch. He's patient and matter-of-fact, but doesn't readily articulate why he wants to enrol — his motivation stays fuzzy.

profiles.name: Santosh
profiles.occupation: runs his own event-management company
profiles.label: Earnest Santosh from Agra, event-company owner
profiles.description: This is Santosh from Agra, UP, a graduate who's spent around 18 years in event management and now runs his own event company; he was in corporates earlier and the business took a beating around Corona. He wants to take the business online and pick up new skills so he can market and grow it across India himself rather than staying local. He's earnest but hard to read — mostly agreeing along, with a few basic questions like whether placements are in India or abroad and whether recordings count for attendance.

profiles.name: Unnamed
profiles.occupation: mustard-oil business owner (company director)
profiles.label: Guarded mustard-oil business owner from Assam
profiles.description: This is a mustard oil business owner from Assam — director of their own company for the last four years, and before that about seven years as a laboratory instructor in the government sector. They're guarded, refusing to disclose business income, and quite price-sensitive: they compare the fee to cheaper competitors, call it too high, and like to bargain for extra time on decisions. They want to build a stronger career and are exploring how to do it.

profiles.name: Devanshu
profiles.occupation: professional footballer (plays for foreign clubs)
profiles.education: Completed 12th via NIOS, planning BBA then MBA
profiles.label: Terse Devanshu from Aligarh, professional footballer
profiles.description: This is Devanshu (name garbled) from Aligarh, UP, who says he's a professional footballer currently playing for foreign clubs. He just completed 12th through the NIOS board and plans to do a BBA and then an MBA, with marketing as his interest. He registered only the day before and hasn't read anything about it, and any money conversation depends on his parents, who are hard to reach. He's a man of few words — short answers, barely any questions beyond 'what will be the benefit'.

profiles.name: Sommli
profiles.education: 2024 graduate, preparing for a competitive exam
profiles.label: Hesitant Sommli, 2024 graduate waiting on exams
profiles.description: Sommli is a 2024 graduate who is currently preparing for a competitive exam and waiting for results. He is hesitant about committing and mentions needing to discuss the decision with his parents. He is price-sensitive about the fee and wants to upskill to improve his job prospects.

profiles.name: Prachi
profiles.label: Hesitant Prachi, polite caller battling audio issues
profiles.description: Prachi is a prospective student attempting to join the call but experiences audio difficulties. She repeatedly thanks the counsellor, indicating politeness. No details about her current employment or academic enrolment are provided. Her demeanor appears hesitant and courteous.

profiles.name: Mukesh
profiles.education: Recent graduate, Centurion University
profiles.label: Hesitant Mukesh from Odisha, recent graduate
profiles.description: Mukesh is a recent graduate from Centurion University in Odisha, currently not employed. He is cautious about the fee and wants to confirm with his parents before paying. He is eager to improve his job prospects and get placement assistance. He comes across as hesitant and detail-oriented during the call.

profiles.name: Arishya
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Arishya from Hyderabad, ex-banking professional
profiles.description: Arishya is a recent banking professional from Hyderabad who is currently not working and preparing for an investment-banking internship. She is price-sensitive, asks many follow-up questions, and wants to upskill for a career move. She is eager for placement support but needs clarity on fees and schedule.

profiles.name: Satyaajit
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Satyajit from Odisha, recent graduate
profiles.description: Satyaajit is a recent graduate from Odisha who is currently not working or studying. He is chatty and somewhat hesitant, repeatedly asking about fee structures and details. He wants to transition into a new field and boost his career prospects. He appears price-sensitive and is seeking a clear pathway forward.

profiles.name: Faraz
profiles.education: Just finished BBA, awaiting result
profiles.label: Scattered Faraz from Lucknow, fresh out of BBA
profiles.description: This is Faraz from Lucknow, UP. He has just finished his BBA — last paper done, still waiting on the result and degree — and isn't working yet. His family runs a property dealing and builder business and his stated goal is to do something around that, though he comes across confused and scattered on the call. He's agreeable but non-committal by nature — the sort who wants the PDF on WhatsApp before he'll confirm anything.

profiles.name: Geetika
profiles.education: Recent high-school graduate, commerce stream
profiles.label: Confident Geetika, aspiring entrepreneur
profiles.description: Geetika is a recent high-school graduate from commerce who won a national business competition and served as logistics head for a MUN conference. She is currently not working or studying, but she is analytical, confident and eager to build skills as she prepares to launch her own startup. She seeks peer learning and industry connections to fill a market gap she has spotted for businesses.

profiles.name: Jay
profiles.education: Recent high-school graduate, integrated MBA starting in two months
profiles.label: Eager Jay from Punjab, integrated MBA starting soon
profiles.description: Jay is a recent high-school graduate from Bernala, Punjab who is about to start a five-year integrated MBA program in two months. He is currently not working or studying, and he sounds a bit nervous but eager to pick up useful skills to boost his future career. He wants a useful skill before college begins. His personality on the call is hesitant yet enthusiastic about learning new things.

profiles.name: Prospect
profiles.label: Hesitant prospect amid a family medical emergency
profiles.description: This is a polite, hesitant prospective student who is currently not working or studying, dealing with a family medical emergency. They seem interested in exploring options for their career. No specific job or academic background is mentioned.

profiles.name: Kritika
profiles.education: Recent graduate
profiles.label: Eager Kritika in Chandigarh, exploring after Teleperformance
profiles.description: Kritika is a recent graduate from Singrude who now lives in Chandigarh without her parents. She just left a six-month role at Teleperformance and is currently not working while she explores new opportunities. She comes across as eager and a bit nervous, asking about fees and emphasizing that she hopes to boost her career and land placements.

profiles.name: Parth
profiles.education: Commerce student, just passed 12th, sat CUET
profiles.label: Sharp Parth from Delhi, CUET-bound commerce student
profiles.description: This is Parth (name garbled in the audio) from Delhi, a commerce student who just passed 12th and has given CUET for college admission. He's aiming for a North Campus DU college and eventually CFA, seeing this as a side hustle to build skills alongside his finance ambitions. He's a sharp kid who asks pointed questions about ROI and how much it is actually worth. Money is parent-controlled — his parents aren't on board yet, and he can't spend anything without checking with his father first.

profiles.name: Kaushik
profiles.education: Fresh 12th passout from a boarding school, awaiting integrated BBA-MBA
profiles.label: Chatty Kaushik, boarding-school achiever awaiting BBA-MBA
profiles.description: This is Kaushik, a fresh 12th passout from a top boarding school (sounds like Assam Valley School), waiting to join an integrated BBA-MBA. He's an achiever type — school sports at national level, MUNs, debating, a rank-one business workshop — and chats happily about his accomplishments. His focus is management, and he wants to build skills for the future. His parents run a business and control the money; he can't commit to anything without phoning them at their shop first.

profiles.name: Adarsh
profiles.label: 29 year old quiet Adarsh from Delhi
profiles.description: This is Adarsh, 29, living with his parents in Delhi. What he does currently never comes through clearly — no job or college mentioned. He doesn't seem aware that this is paid. Quiet, brief answers, hard to read.

profiles.name: Anish
profiles.education: BBA, just finished, from a small town in Odisha
profiles.label: Honest Anish from Odisha, BBA done, CAT ahead
profiles.description: This is Anish from a small town in Odisha. He finished his BBA literally a week ago and isn't working — the real plan is an MBA in finance with CAT prep ahead. He's refreshingly honest and articulate, admitting he has no particular reason for this beyond making his profile stronger for B-school admissions. He won't commit to anything without his parents and needs to check with them before putting down any money.

profiles.name: Benita
profiles.education: PhD in cancer immune biology, NII Delhi
profiles.label: Thorough Dr. Benita, biotech PhD seeking work-from-home
profiles.description: This is Dr. Benita, a PhD in cancer immune biology from NII Delhi. She spent 2.5+ years as a senior analyst at a biotech company working on the AI side, but resigned over her brother's ill health and is now on a break, looking for opportunities. She's a mum to a young daughter and is firm that she needs a work-from-home role with decent pay. Polite but thorough — she asked about recordings, placement timelines, scholarships, and whether someone with zero coding background can cope.

profiles.name: Kashish
profiles.education: fresh graduate (exact degree unclear)
profiles.label: Price-sensitive Kashish, fresh graduate, driver's son
profiles.description: This is Kashish, a fresh graduate (the exact degree got garbled in the audio) who isn't working; his father earns a living as a driver. He found out about it online and is clearly price-sensitive, the kind to weigh cheaper or offline alternatives before spending anything. He won't make a money decision without discussing it with his family first. Short, deferential answers throughout.

profiles.name: Aryan
profiles.education: 12th passout
profiles.label: Parent-dependent Aryan, 12th passout from Instagram reels
profiles.description: This is Aryan Singh, a 12th passout who found out about it through Instagram reels and isn't studying or working yet. His parents — a pharmacist father and teacher mother — hold the purse strings, and he has no idea about the fees himself. He says he's interested, but any money decision rests entirely on his parents, with his mum deferring to his dad. Polite, brief, very much a yes-ma'am kid.

profiles.name: Priyansh
profiles.education: B.Tech in electronics (incoming), likely at VIT; just cleared 12th with 85.2%
profiles.label: 18 year old chatty Priyansh from Patna
profiles.description: This is Priyansh from Patna, 18, who just cleared 12th with 85.2% and qualified VITEEE — he's headed for a B.Tech in electronics, most likely at VIT. He's a hustler for his age: freelanced in data analysis, data entry, transcription and blogging for 8-10k a month, and has started teaching himself C, C++ and Python. Chatty and enthusiastic on the call, but from a middle-class family — his father balks at spending around 50k on top of upcoming college fees.

profiles.name: Abhishek
profiles.label: Methodical Abhishek, anxious about every payment detail
profiles.description: This is Abhishek Chauhan, back for a second session after an earlier counselling call with a different counsellor. He never reveals what he does — any money would come from his parents' account, which suggests he is not earning yet. Methodical and a little anxious: he grills the counsellor on payment deadlines, refundability, batch size, placement rates, and whether recorded classes count for attendance, double-checking every detail step by step.

profiles.name: Arpita
profiles.education: 12th-pass medical-stream student preparing for NEET
profiles.label: Curious Arpita from Mohali, preparing for NEET
profiles.description: This is Arpita from Mohali, Chandigarh, a 12th-pass medical-stream student currently preparing for NEET. She wants an MBBS eventually but is after a skill that could become passive income on the side. Her mother is a teacher and her father runs a flour mill, and everything hinges on their permission and funding — she only told her mum minutes before the call, and she won't spend anything without consulting her parents first. Sweet and curious, asking about packages and placements.

profiles.name: Prachi
profiles.education: master's in business analytics and marketing, Pune institute
profiles.label: Hesitant Prachi, restarting career after medical break
profiles.description: This is Prachi, who did her master's in business analytics and marketing from a Pune institute and has about two years' experience in finance firms — relationship manager at a housing finance company, then lead generation at Chronica Finance. She quit her job for medical treatment and is now looking to restart her career, already knowing a little SQL and Power BI. Soft-spoken and hesitant on a patchy phone connection with no laptop at home. She leans on her sister for big decisions and wouldn't commit to anything without talking to her first.

profiles.name: Ankita
profiles.education: BA in political science, then LLB from Delhi University
profiles.label: Articulate Ankita from Bihar, UPSC aspirant seeking backup
profiles.description: This is Ankita from Bihar, a humanities-and-law grad — BA in political science, then LLB from Delhi University during lockdown — who has spent the last four-five years on UPSC prep with repeated failures. She is now hunting for backup options. A smart, articulate questioner: she probes how non-tech students fare in placements and how much her gap years would hurt. Very risk-aware though — she has a PCS exam on 26 July, frets that the 4K is non-refundable if she clears it, and would want to consult her parents before committing to anything.

profiles.name: Shiva
profiles.label: Hesitant Shiva from Hyderabad, jobless after UPSC prep
profiles.description: This is Shiva from Hyderabad, a 2021 B.Sc Visual Communication grad from Vel Tech, Chennai. He did about 1.5 years in US healthcare revenue cycle management, but has spent the last two years jobless while preparing for UPSC exams. He's hesitant and budget-conscious - openly says 'I'm jobless', asks about starting salaries, and tends to just go quiet when pressed too hard. He wants to move out of non-IT into something with less math and more business strategy, since his arts background steers him that way.

profiles.name: Nishtha
profiles.label: 21 year old parent-dependent Nishtha from Hoshiarpur
profiles.description: This is Nishtha Narula, 21, from Hoshiarpur, Punjab. She never reveals a job or college - what's clear is she isn't the one paying: her sister told her to ask their parents, and her father was busy at the shop when she tried calling. She's hesitant and parent-dependent, says she's interested but 'a little bit unsure' without family sign-off.

profiles.name: Shweta
profiles.label: Tough Shweta, sales pro returning from career break
profiles.description: This is Shweta, a sales pro with eight years across hospitality, Byju's, a US-based firm and TeamViewer, which she left in January 2025. She's currently on a career break as the mother of a one-year-old and wants to re-enter with a domain change, eyeing roles like product manager. She's confident and tough on the call - demands a detailed syllabus, asks for proof of placed students, pushes back that her resume is already ATS-friendly, and flatly refuses to be rushed into deciding. She's the type to check Bitsom reviews, talk to Masai alumni and her husband before paying anything.

profiles.name: Samuel
profiles.education: Just finished B.Com in finance and information systems, St Aloysius, Mangalore
profiles.label: 21 year old eager Samuel from Mangalore
profiles.description: This is Samuel from Mangalore, 21, who finished his B.Com in finance and information systems at St Aloysius literally last week, results two days before the call. He's not working - he's planning a master's abroad in Poland or Lithuania and has about ten months to fill, so he wants to build skills and pad his resume, with a long-term dream of starting his own business. He's enthusiastic and compliant on the call, answering every question eagerly, though he runs big decisions past his parents.

profiles.name: Taufiq
profiles.label: Eager Taufiq from Kashmir, family construction business
profiles.description: This is Taufiq from Jammu and Kashmir, a 12th pass-out (PCB, 69%) with no college or job mentioned; his family runs a construction business. He wants to pick up skills that could help the family business grow. He's strikingly eager and unquestioning, with his parents at home and seemingly on board. His only real doubt is whether recorded lectures count for attendance when he misses live classes.

profiles.name: Ankita
profiles.label: Unsure Ankita, 12th passout without college admission
profiles.description: This is Ankita, who just passed 12th with 65% and hasn't secured a college admission yet — she is exploring options to figure out what she is good at. She is entirely dependent on her father, who feels she is too young and should research more before committing. She asks a genuinely good question about whether this makes sense with zero experience. She is soft-spoken and unsure throughout.

profiles.name: Kunal
profiles.education: Just finished BBA, Bhavan's Vivekananda College; planning an MBA next
profiles.label: Chatty Kunal from Hyderabad, cricketer planning his MBA
profiles.description: This is Kunal Dostwani, originally from Delhi but based in Gachibowli, Hyderabad, where he moved to play cricket — he has been a cricketer for 14 years. He just finished his BBA at Bhavan's Vivekananda College a month ago and plans to do his MBA there next. His dad is a chartered accountant and his mom runs a sports retail shop, which Kunal dreams of scaling into a big organization. He is friendly and chatty but admits he knows little beyond one college subject, and money decisions go through his father.

profiles.name: Renuka
profiles.label: Thoughtful Renuka from Nagpur, developer restarting after maternity
profiles.description: This is Renuka Pandey from Nagpur, a BSc Computer Science and MCA graduate who worked about 2.6 years as a .NET developer before going on a maternity break in 2020 — she has two daughters and has been out of work for 5-6 years. She wants to fill the gap and switch from backend coding to a client-facing role. She is thoughtful and detail-oriented, asking sharp questions about mentor allocation, EMI interest, when she'd be job-ready, and how her career gap will play in interviews. She wouldn't spend anything without discussing it with her husband first.

profiles.name: Aryan
profiles.label: Directionless Aryan from Bhiwandi, engineering college ahead
profiles.description: This is Aryan from Bhiwandi, a village kid whose father Sanjay works in the BMC. He just passed 12th with 57% and plans to join engineering college in a couple of months, but hasn't applied anywhere yet. He's eager but directionless, literally says he's 'going with the flow', and very parent-dependent — any money decision means pulling his father in. He wants to build skills and make up for his low 12th marks so he can land a high-paying job.

profiles.name: Rohan
profiles.label: Inquisitive B.Com grad from Guwahati, weighing UK MBA
profiles.description: This guy is from Guwahati, Assam and finished his B.Com from Mithibai College, Mumbai a couple of months ago. He's neither working nor studying right now, he's weighing an MBA from the UK in the January window, and otherwise plans a startup or joining his dad's business. He's polite but quite inquisitive, asking about batch size, hands-on content, top performers, UK placements and discounts, and admits he's not tech-friendly with no coding background. He wants to build up some skills before the MBA, and says his parents are already on board with the idea.

profiles.name: Rudra
profiles.education: Finished integrated MSc IT, planning a master's at Gujarat University
profiles.label: Vague Rudra from Gujarat, exploring after MSc IT
profiles.description: This is Rudra from Gujarat, who finished an integrated software development degree (MSc IT) this year and is planning a master's at Gujarat University. He's not working, got no placement, and applied after seeing an Instagram reel. He's hesitant and vague about goals, admits he barely researched it, and can't pay anything without his parents.

profiles.name: Sonu
profiles.label: Passive Sonu, drifting ex-hotel operations executive
profiles.description: This is Sonu, who recently left a job as an operations executive at a hotel and is doing nothing at the moment. He hasn't looked at the fees or website. He's passive and hard to draw out, giving minimal one-word answers, and seems more comfortable in Hindi. He signed up vaguely hoping to learn something new.

profiles.name: Soumya
profiles.education: B.Com graduate, did a one-month internship at a CA firm in auditing and tax compliance
profiles.label: 21 year old hesitant Soumya, B.Com graduate
profiles.description: This is Soumya, 21, a B.Com graduate who did a one-month internship at a CA firm in auditing and tax compliance, and isn't working right now. Her father runs his own dry-fruit wholesale business, and she's entirely dependent on her parents for the fee decision. She wants to become more hireable and learn how the real industry works. She's soft-spoken and hesitant, asks about EMI versus one-time payment, and she just lost her cousin brother, so it's the wrong time to discuss money with her parents.

profiles.name: Arjun
profiles.education: Just passed 12th, planning BBA or a UI/UX design course
profiles.label: 17 year old decisive Arjun, national rifle shooter
profiles.description: This is Arjun, 17, who just passed 12th and is a national-level rifle shooter (10m and 50m). He's between school and college — planning BBA or a UI/UX design course — and frankly admits he signed up because he was bored and likes trying different things, like learning Spanish. He's casual, honest and surprisingly decisive, and his parents already know about it. Easy-going kid, not one for hard questions.

profiles.name: Raghav
profiles.education: 12th pass-out and CA aspirant, gave CA Foundation in May, hunting for a B.Com college
profiles.label: Ambitious Raghav, CA aspirant hunting B.Com colleges
profiles.description: This is Raghav, a 12th pass-out and CA aspirant who just gave his CA Foundation attempt in May and is hunting for a B.Com college. He's ambitious and articulate — he wants something extraordinary to stand out among CA peers and a way to differentiate himself; long term he wants to start something of his own. He comes across as driven and clear about his plan.

profiles.name: Manan
profiles.education: B.Com grad from Narsee Monjee, appeared for CA Final and awaiting results
profiles.label: Chatty Manan from Mumbai, awaiting CA Final results
profiles.description: This is Manan from Mumbai (Goregaon), a B.Com grad from Narsee Monjee who has appeared for his CA Final and is waiting for results. His dad is a CA at NESCO, and Manan's plan is to start his own consultancy someday, so he's after knowledge and networking. He's chatty and genuinely engaged — plays football and badminton, asks practical questions about exam format, recordings, paid tool access and hands-on versus theory. But he's financially parent-dependent, and his father baulks at spending ~90k, saying CA is enough.

profiles.name: Yashasvi
profiles.education: Just gave 12th boards with 82.2%, preparing for CUET, leaning towards finance, no college decided
profiles.label: Quiet Yashasvi from Noida, mother doing the talking
profiles.description: This is Yashasvi (name garbled in the audio) Gupta from Noida. She just gave her 12th boards with 82.2% and is preparing for CUET, with no college decided yet — she's leaning towards finance and admits she's mostly exploring. She's quiet and lets her mother do almost all the talking, with the mother asking the practical questions about timings and eligibility. Signing up was a joint family decision rather than any strong personal pull.

profiles.name: Mishika
profiles.education: Just finished 12th in commerce with 75%, waiting to join college, wants to study economics
profiles.label: 17 year old dad-dependent Mishika from Delhi
profiles.description: This is Mishika, 17, from Delhi. She just finished 12th in commerce with 75% and is waiting to join college, wanting to study economics and eventually start her own business. She found it on a YouTube channel and is genuinely keen, but she's completely financially dependent — she can't pay a rupee without her dad, who's only home at night. She's polite but firm, the type who doesn't budge under pressure.

profiles.name: Charulatha
profiles.education: Finished 12th in 2020, applying for BBA admissions after years of NEET prep
profiles.label: Timid Charulatha, drifting after years of NEET prep
profiles.description: This is Charulatha (name garbled). She finished 12th back in 2020, spent the last three years preparing for NEET without it working out, and is now applying for BBA admissions while exploring other options. She's quiet and timid, doesn't know the fees at all, and depends entirely on her parents, who are usually busy and hard to reach. She found it on social media and comes across as drifting rather than chasing a specific goal.

profiles.name: Rituparna
profiles.occupation: Most recently a retail store manager (previously in sales at Flipkart)
profiles.label: Decisive Rituparna from Odisha, retail manager switching tracks
profiles.description: This is Rituparna from Odisha, an MBA in marketing and finance who has worked in sales at Flipkart and most recently as a retail store manager. She's been on sick leave since November and has used the break to decide she wants to switch tracks rather than go back. She comes from a business family and is clear-headed and decisive, asking pointed questions about placement evaluations and whether classes are truly live. She sees gaining new skills as the missing piece if she wants to do something big.
